name: Task Master
description: AI-powered task-management system that parses PRDs into a dependency-aware task graph and plugs into Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, Roo, VS Code, and Lovable via MCP.

description.value: PRD-to-task-graph planning layer that drops into AI coding agents via MCP, with an optional TDD autopilot that enforces a RED/GREEN/COMMIT cycle.

website.comment: docs.task-master.dev is the canonical public entry point; repo homepage field points to tryhamster.com (company site)

creator.comment: LICENSE credits Eyal Toledano and Ralph Khreish; Jason Zhou frequently credited as an early co-author in community write-ups

first-release.comment: Repo created 2025-03-04; npm package launched in March 2025

license.comment: MIT license with an added Commons Clause that prohibits reselling the software or offering it as a hosted service

pricing-model.comment: Free to install and use; users pay for their own model API keys. No paid tier from the project itself.

open-source.comment: Source is public and free to use, but the Commons Clause restriction on reselling disqualifies the license from OSI-approved open source

covered-phases.comment: PRD parsing (spec) → task generation (planning/tasks) → implementation via task status transitions; testing is a distinct RED phase in the TDD autopilot

methodology-style.comment: Core loop is plan-from-PRD then execute tasks; strict-tdd applies only when the optional autopilot TDD workflow is used

opinionated.comment: Tasks, subtasks, dependencies, and tags can be freely added/reordered; the default flow does not force a strict sequence

ceremony-level.comment: Requires a PRD and produces a structured task graph but skips heavier artifacts like ADRs, architecture docs, or review gates

mechanization-level.comment: 36-tool MCP surface plus a full CLI; PRD parsing, task expansion, dependency validation, and status transitions are single-command

hitl-checkpoints.comment: User drives task selection via `next`, reviews expansions, and transitions status per subtask

approval-gates.comment: Default workflow expects human review between subtasks; autopilot still validates RED/GREEN phase transitions but does not require spec/plan approval

autonomous-e2e.comment: The autopilot PREFLIGHT → BRANCH_SETUP → SUBTASK_LOOP → FINALIZE pipeline can run a full TDD cycle without human steps when driven by an agent

autonomy-level.comment: Default configuration has phase-level human approvals; autopilot can push this to 4 when left to run unattended

rollback-support.comment: autopilot_abort cleans workflow state but preserves the branch and code; there is no built-in artifact/code rollback command

supported-agents.comment: README lists Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, Lovable, Roo Code, Q Developer CLI, and Claude Code as first-party integrations; Lovable/Q Developer captured under `other`

editor-integrations.comment: VS Code and the CLI are first-party; Lovable is the web integration. No documented JetBrains or Neovim integration.

model-agnostic.comment: Configurable per-role providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Perplexity, xAI, OpenRouter, Mistral, Groq, Azure OpenAI, Ollama

mcp-support.comment: Ships an MCP server exposing 36 tools; configurable core/standard/all tool bundles

artifact-formats.comment: tasks.json is the authoritative store; individual task files can be generated as markdown with metadata headers

artifacts-covered.comment: PRDs (specs) are the input, tasks/subtasks are the primary output, and a complexity report acts as a lightweight plan

project-layout.value: .taskmaster/ directory at project root containing config.json, state.json, tasks/, reports/, templates/, and docs/

artifacts-in-git.comment: .taskmaster/ lives inside the repo; autopilot creates task-{id} branches and commits per GREEN phase

language-agnostic.comment: Task definitions are prose; no language-specific tooling or codegen

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Default enforcement is instructional via .cursor/rules and CLAUDE.md; autopilot adds a hard test-must-fail/test-must-pass gate

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Non-autopilot workflow allows ad-hoc jumping between tasks and statuses; autopilot does enforce RED→GREEN→COMMIT ordering when engaged

artifact-validation.comment: Schema compliance, dependency validity, and circular-dependency checks are built in; `validate_dependencies` and `fix_dependencies` are dedicated tools

memory-implementation.comment: Structured state lives in tasks.json (kv/JSON store); long-form context lives in CLAUDE.md and .cursor/rules (markdown files)

persistent-memory.comment: tasks.json, state.json, and reports/ persist across sessions; tags partition task lists for parallel workstreams

project-context-files.comment: Init generates CLAUDE.md and .cursor/rules; repo also ships .kiro/ and .claude-plugin/ configurations

cost-tracking.comment: No token or cost tracking surfaced in docs (checked 2026-04-21); costs accrue against whichever model provider the user configures

run-history.comment: No structured run history or trace store documented; autopilot tracks current workflow state only

telemetry-opt.comment: Telemetry is not mentioned in the documentation or README as of 2026-04-21; could not determine whether any exists

custom-templates.comment: Init creates .taskmaster/templates/example_prd.txt; users can add their own PRD templates under that directory

sub-agents.comment: No persona or sub-agent system; the research/main/fallback model roles are model slots, not user-defined personas

marketplace.comment: No public marketplace or shared template registry as of 2026-04-21

install-mechanism.comment: Primary install is `npm i -g task-master-ai`; MCP configs point editors at the same package

setup-time.comment: npm install is quick but MCP wiring, model API keys, and first PRD parse push first-run past 5 minutes in practice

self-hosted.comment: Runs fully locally; only outbound calls are to the user's chosen model provider

requires-account.comment: No Task Master account. Users do need their own API keys for whichever model provider they configure.

git-worktree-support.comment: Autopilot creates task-{id} branches but does not use or document git worktrees

documentation-quality.comment: Dedicated docs site with Getting Started, Capabilities, Best Practices, TDD Workflow, and provider sections; marked v1 with ongoing weekly updates

active-maintenance.comment: Last release v0.43.1 on 2026-03-31 and last push 2026-03-31, both within 90 days of the 2026-04-21 research date

example-projects.comment: Ships example_prd.txt template; docs site includes PRD creation walkthroughs

community-channel.comment: Discord invite linked from the docs landing page
